clinical_criteria: Age 4-35 years at time of principal asthma diagnosis
At least 2 asthma codes on different days
Must have inhaled corticosteroid use while ages 4-35
Must have acute asthma exacerbation code or wheezing between check in/check out dates of inpatient stay
Must have oral/iv steroids between check in/check out dates of inpatient stay
Oral/iv steroids are limited to WIZ orders
For emergency hospitalizations: absolute difference between check-in date (of inpatient stay) and entry date of emergency note is 1 day
Route must be (IV, IM, or PO) OR have a dose/strength (MG|milligram|miligram) AND Route not like intranasal|topical|inhaled
algorithm_summary: This phenotype identifies asthma patients aged 4-35 who respond to inhaled corticosteroids (ICS). It requires at least 2 asthma diagnoses (493.xx) on different days and excludes patients with various conditions like cystic fibrosis, immunodeficiency, COPD, and other respiratory/systemic diseases. The algorithm focuses on asthma exacerbations requiring hospitalization with oral/IV steroid treatment, and specifically tracks the use of inhaled corticosteroids during the specified age range. It includes detailed medication specifications for both oral/IV steroids and inhaled corticosteroids, with specific route and dosing requirements.
